2026-07-13.title: Gunmen Kill Ecuadorean Drug Lord's Brother in Fake Police Raid
2026-07-13.facts: Men disguised as police officers murdered David Macías in Ecuador over the weekend, in what authorities are calling a targeted assassination. Macías was the brother of notorious drug lord José Adolfo Macías—known as "El Fito"—who's currently awaiting trial in the United States.

2026-07-13.title: Japan Building New Intelligence Agency With Western Support
2026-07-13.facts: Japan is breaking with decades of post-World War II security limits to build a new intelligence agency, and it's getting help from the West. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is driving the effort, citing mounting threats from both Russia and China as Japan rethinks its pacifist stance.

2026-07-13.title: Senator Lindsey Graham Dies
2026-07-13.facts: South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham has died. The sharp-witted Republican was known as one of the Senate's most mercurial figures—able to repair fractured relationships with political rivals and insert himself into nearly every major legislative battle during his long tenure.

2026-07-13.title: Five Killed in Illinois Family Shooting
2026-07-13.facts: A shooting in southern Illinois has left five members of one family dead and two others wounded. Illinois State Police announced Sunday that two teenage suspects are now in custody following the deadly incident.

2026-07-13.title: Supreme Court Seeks Bigger Security Budget
2026-07-13.facts: The Supreme Court is heading to Capitol Hill with an unusual request—and an unusual pair of messengers. Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Elena Kagan are scheduled to testify before Congress about the court's request for more than $200 million in additional security funding.

2026-07-13.title: Oil Spikes as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz
2026-07-13.facts: Oil prices surged as much as 5% on Monday after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to shipping. The move comes amid escalating attacks between the U.S.

2026-07-13.title: Taiwan's Chipmaker Posts Record Revenue Ahead of Earnings
2026-07-13.facts: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Corporation just reported a record month for revenue, with June sales climbing 67% to $13.2 billion. It's a striking number that underscores how insatiable demand remains for the advanced chips TSMC produces.

2026-07-13.title: South Korean Chipmaker Plunges After U.S. Debut
2026-07-13.facts: SK Hynix saw its local shares in Seoul plummet 15% on Monday, just one day after a highly successful debut of its U.S. depositary receipts.

2026-07-13.title: Mortgage Rates Jump on Iran Tensions, But Homebuyers Keep Coming
2026-07-13.facts: Mortgage rates have climbed in response to the escalating conflict in Iran, yet housing demand is defying the spike. Pending home sales rose to nearly 64,000 last month, up from just over 61,000 in the same period last year.

2026-07-13.title: Record-Breaking Sale Sees 49ers Minority Owner Buy Seattle Seahawks
2026-07-13.facts: A group led by Niners limited partner Vinod Khosla has agreed to purchase the Seattle Seahawks for $9.612 billion, sources tell ESPN—making it the most expensive sports franchise sale in history. The price tag shatters previous records and underscores just how valuable NFL ownership has become.

2026-07-13.title: Warriors Add Vogel as Kerr's Top Assistant
2026-07-13.facts: The Warriors are bringing in Frank Vogel as their new associate head coach, filling the hole left by Terry Stotts' departure. Vogel will work directly under Steve Kerr, adding another experienced voice to Golden State's coaching staff.

2026-07-13.title: Claude Cowork's Real Sweet Spot: The Work You're Relieved to Offload
2026-07-13.facts: Anthropic's letting slip what Claude Cowork is actually being used for in the wild, and it's not the flashy autonomous projects you'd expect. The real action is in what they're calling "mundane office work nobody wants to own"—the spreadsheet reconciliations, the invoice chasing, the data entry that sits in everyone's queue until Friday afternoon panic sets in.

2026-07-14.title: Yemen's Houthi Strikes Spread to Saudi Arabia
2026-07-14.facts: Yemen's Houthi fighters launched missiles at Saudi Arabia, targeting Abha airport in retaliation for strikes on Sanaa's airport. The Iran-backed group blamed Saudi Arabia for the attack on Yemen's capital and struck back within hours.

2026-07-14.title: Japan Launches First Centralized Intelligence Agency Since WWII
2026-07-14.facts: Japan is building a centralized intelligence agency for the first time since World War II, a sweeping shift driven by threats from Russia and China. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is leading the effort with help from Western allies who are advising on how to structure the new organization.

2026-07-14.title: Supreme Court Justices Head to Capitol Seeking Security Funding
2026-07-14.facts: Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett are making a rare trip to Capitol Hill Wednesday to ask Congress for millions more in security funding. They'll be the first justices to testify before lawmakers since 2019, and the timing's no accident—threats against the court have escalated sharply in recent years.

2026-07-14.title: Immigration Agent Kills Man in Maine Vehicle Shooting
2026-07-14.facts: A federal immigration agent shot and killed a man during an encounter in Biddeford, Maine Monday morning. The victim has been identified as Joan Sebastian Guerrero, who died in a vehicle during the confrontation with ICE.

2026-07-14.title: Health Department Reverses $10 Billion Funding Freeze for Five States
2026-07-14.facts: The Department of Health and Human Services has backed down from a controversial decision to freeze $10 billion in funding for five Democratic-led states. The reversal came after the administration faced repeated legal setbacks in court challenges to the funding pause.

2026-07-14.title: JPMorgan Delivers Biggest Profit Beat in Five Years
2026-07-14.facts: JPMorgan just posted its largest profit beat in half a decade, powered by a surge in revenue from equity trading. The bank's earnings smashed Wall Street's expectations, driven by a record-breaking quarter for overall revenue.

2026-07-14.title: Bank of America Stock Drops Despite Earnings Beat
2026-07-14.facts: Bank of America's shares tumbled in pre-market trading even after the bank reported a fifteen percent jump in revenue compared to last year. The drop highlights a disconnect between strong financial performance and investor sentiment that's become increasingly common this earnings season.

2026-07-14.title: California Leads Lawsuit to Block Paramount Warner Bros Merger
2026-07-14.facts: California and eleven other states are moving to block the proposed merger between Paramount and Warner Bros, setting up a major legal fight over media consolidation. The lawsuit comes from states where both studios have significant operations, with California home to both companies' headquarters.

2026-07-14.title: City Hands NYPD Parking Lot to Land Trust for Affordable Housing
2026-07-14.facts: The Mamdani administration just awarded its first city-owned site to a community land trust, converting an NYPD parking lot in the East Village into 131 affordable apartments. The Cooper Square Committee, Housing Works, and Spatial Equity are partnering with This Land is Ours Community Land Trust to transform the police lot at 324 East 5th Street into housing, a senior center, and community space.

2026-07-14.title: Walker Wins Home Run Derby in Dramatic Finish
2026-07-14.facts: Jordan Walker just claimed the 2026 Home Run Derby title in spectacular fashion, homering on his final six swings to overtake hometown favorite Kyle Schwarber. The Cardinals slugger's late surge made him the first player from St.

2026-07-14.title: Lakers Acquire Walker Kessler in Trade
2026-07-14.facts: Walker Kessler is heading to the Los Angeles Lakers in a deal that cost the franchise both money and draft picks, giving the team a much-needed big man as it begins life without LeBron James. Kessler spoke to reporters after the trade and didn't mince words about his commitment.

2026-07-14.title: Claude Speaks Different Values in Different Languages
2026-07-14.facts: Anthropic just published research showing that Claude doesn't hold the same values when you talk to it in different languages—and the differences are bigger than you'd think. The company tested how Claude responds to ethical questions across languages like English, Spanish, Japanese, and Hindi, and found that the model's answers shift depending on which language you're using.

2026-07-15.title: India Expels Bangladeshi Immigrants, Sparking Border Crisis
2026-07-15.facts: India's pushing out thousands of Bangladeshi immigrants it says are in the country illegally, and Bangladesh isn't having it. The mass deportations have left people stranded along the border, caught between two countries that are increasingly at odds over who belongs where.

2026-07-15.title: Russian Strikes Hit Odesa as Infrastructure War Escalates
2026-07-15.facts: Russian bombs continue falling on Odesa, killing three people in the latest wave of attacks targeting Ukraine's critical port infrastructure. The strikes are part of an escalating pattern in recent days, with both Russia and Ukraine launching attacks on each other's facilities for shipping goods.

2026-07-15.title: China's Top Space Official Purged Over Corruption and Sex Charges
2026-07-15.facts: Beijing just expelled one of its most powerful officials from the Communist Party. Ma Xingrui, who once ran China's space program and later led the Xinjiang region, has been purged over corruption and sex-related charges.

2026-07-15.title: Bangkok Pub Fire Death Toll Climbs to 32
2026-07-15.facts: The death toll from a Bangkok pub fire has risen to 32, with 15 people still fighting for their lives in intensive care. Officials confirmed two more deaths on Tuesday, bringing the grim count higher as doctors work to save 30 others still hospitalized.

2026-07-15.title: Pleasure Boat Sinks Near Alcatraz, Killing One
2026-07-15.facts: A pleasure boat capsized in San Francisco Bay near Alcatraz on Tuesday, leaving one person dead and three others still missing. Sixteen passengers were pulled from the water and survived the accident, which happened in one of the bay's busiest tourist corridors.

2026-07-15.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Shifts Focus to the Strait of Hormuz
2026-07-15.facts: The military confrontation between the United States and Iran has entered a new and potentially more dangerous phase, with American forces now concentrating on the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump has resumed hostilities after the brief ceasefire collapsed, and the narrow waterway—through which a fifth of the world's oil passes—has become the focal point of operations.

2026-07-15.title: Inflation Cools to 3.5 Percent as Iran Conflict Pause Offered Brief Relief
2026-07-15.facts: Consumer prices climbed just 3.5 percent in June compared to a year earlier, marking the slowest pace of inflation in months. Even more striking: prices actually fell from May to June, posting their biggest monthly drop since 2020.

2026-07-15.title: New Fed Chair Warsh Pledges to Lower Inflation Without Revealing His Plan
2026-07-15.facts: Kevin Warsh made his debut before Congress as Federal Reserve chairman, promising to bring inflation down but offering virtually no detail on how he intends to do it. In his first testimony on Capitol Hill, Warsh reiterated the Fed's commitment to taming price pressures but didn't signal whether he supports raising interest rates further or taking a different approach.

2026-07-15.title: Thames Water Returns to Profit After Raising Customer Bills
2026-07-15.facts: Britain's troubled Thames Water has swung back to profitability after hiking what it charges households for water and sewage service. The utility, which serves millions of customers across London and southern England, reported a return to the black in its latest financial results.

2026-07-15.title: BlackRock Hits $15 Trillion Milestone as Profits Surge
2026-07-15.facts: BlackRock just crossed a threshold no asset manager has ever reached before: $15 trillion under management. The world's largest fund manager reported a jump in profits that sent its shares climbing, fueled by investors pouring money into both stocks and bonds.

2026-07-15.title: Bobby Cayre Unloads Meatpacking District Building for $100 Million
2026-07-15.facts: Bobby Cayre just closed the biggest commercial real estate deal to hit city records this week, selling a mixed-use property at 675 Hudson Street in the Meatpacking District for $100 million. The sale topped a busy day of transactions—177 deals totaling $612 million were filed in the 24 hours ending Tuesday afternoon.

2026-07-15.title: Zeckendorf Family Sues Supermarket Heirs Over Stolen Painting
2026-07-15.facts: The Zeckendorf family is accusing the owners of a Pennsylvania supermarket chain of selling a painting they say was stolen from them half a century ago. The lawsuit targets the heirs of Robert and Patricia Weis, who control nearly two-thirds of Weis Markets, over an abstract work by Pierre Soulages that sold at Christie's last fall for nearly $5 million.

2026-07-15.title: Spain Stun France to Reach World Cup Final
2026-07-15.facts: Spain are headed to the World Cup final after dismantling France 2-0 in Tuesday's semifinal, a result that's sent shockwaves through the tournament. France, one of the pre-tournament favorites, collapsed in spectacular fashion, failing to find any rhythm against a Spanish side that controlled the match from start to finish.

2026-07-15.title: Number One Draft Pick Cholowsky Gets Record-Breaking Bonus
2026-07-15.facts: The White Sox have agreed to terms with Roch Cholowsky, the number one overall pick in this year's MLB draft, and they've shattered the record for signing bonuses in the process. Cholowsky's deal reportedly includes a $10.35 million signing bonus, the largest ever handed to a drafted player.

2026-07-15.title: Timberwolves Acquire LaMelo Ball in Blockbuster Deal
2026-07-15.facts: The Timberwolves just made one of the biggest moves of the offseason, landing star point guard LaMelo Ball in what team president Tim Connelly is calling a franchise-altering trade. Connelly says he believes Ball can "elevate" the team's ceiling, signaling Minnesota's all-in on competing for a championship now.

2026-07-15.title: Claude Code Gets a Browser—and That Changes the Autonomous Agent Game
2026-07-15.facts: Anthropic just shipped a built-in browser for Claude Code, and the reason this matters isn't convenience—it's about breaking the Playwright ceiling. Until now, any site that blocked headless automation or used aggressive bot detection forced you to either abandon the task or hack together workarounds with Computer Use.

2026-07-16.title: Argentina Faces FIFA Sanctions Over Falklands Banner
2026-07-16.facts: Argentina's headed for disciplinary action from FIFA after their players celebrated a World Cup semi-final victory over England with a banner supporting the country's territorial claims to the Falkland Islands. The incident occurred immediately after the match, with players displaying the politically charged message during post-game festivities.

2026-07-16.title: Italy Awaits Genoa Bridge Collapse Verdict
2026-07-16.facts: Families of 43 victims are waiting to learn whether anyone will be held accountable for one of Italy's deadliest infrastructure disasters. A verdict is expected soon in the trial over the 2018 Morandi Bridge collapse in Genoa, when a massive section of the motorway bridge crumbled onto railway tracks below.

2026-07-16.title: Pew Poll Shows Global Opinion Shifting Toward China
2026-07-16.facts: More countries now view China favorably than the United States, according to a new global survey that marks a significant shift in international sentiment. The annual Pew Research Center poll found that positive views of China outpaced those of America across the countries surveyed.

2026-07-16.title: Rohingya Boat Disaster Leaves Over 500 Feared Dead
2026-07-16.facts: More than 500 people are feared dead after two boats carrying mostly Rohingya passengers capsized off Myanmar's coast. The International Organization for Migration and the UN refugee agency say the vessels departed Rakhine State in late June, packed with refugees fleeing persecution.

2026-07-16.title: Hate Crime Investigation Opens After Stabbing Near Salt Lake City
2026-07-16.facts: A man is in custody after allegedly stabbing a Muslim employee at a mall near Salt Lake City, and court documents reveal the suspect told investigators he wanted to kill Muslims. The victim, who works at the mall, is hospitalized with multiple stab wounds.

2026-07-16.title: FBI Investigates Air Force One Leak After Security Report
2026-07-16.facts: The FBI is seeking to interview government officials and asking for their phones as part of a leak investigation tied to Air Force One. The probe was launched after The New York Times reported on security concerns surrounding the Qatari-donated jet that President Trump has been using.

2026-07-16.title: Inflation Cools to 3.5 Percent as War Pause Delivered Brief Relief
2026-07-16.facts: Consumer prices rose just 3.5 percent in the year through June, the slowest pace in months, and marked the biggest monthly drop since 2020. The data arrived as welcome news for the White House, which has been battling elevated inflation for years now.

2026-07-16.title: Trump Administration Plans New Tariffs on Brazil
2026-07-16.facts: The White House is preparing to slap new tariffs on Brazil, accusing the country of unfair trade practices. The move comes after the Supreme Court struck down an earlier set of tariffs, forcing the administration to craft a replacement.

2026-07-16.title: Britain Nationalizes Its Last Major Steelworks to Secure Supply
2026-07-16.facts: The British government has taken full ownership of British Steel, including the massive Scunthorpe steelworks, in a move officials are calling vital to national security. The nationalization became official this week under new powers Parliament just passed, marking one of the most significant industrial interventions in years.

2026-07-16.title: UnitedHealth Stock Rallies on Improved Full-Year Outlook
2026-07-16.facts: UnitedHealth just gave Wall Street something it's been craving: good news on the bottom line. The health insurance giant's stock rallied Thursday after management raised its outlook for the full year, pointing to what they're calling "product design changes, improved medical management and better aligned pricing." Translation: they're paying out less in claims while still bringing in premium revenue.

2026-07-16.title: SK Hynix Volatility Shows No Sign of Slowing Down
2026-07-16.facts: If you thought SK Hynix's wild price swings were over, think again. The South Korean chipmaker's stock collapsed 12 percent Thursday, just one day after jumping nearly 9 percent on Wednesday.

2026-07-16.title: City Council Pressures Housing Agency on Mitchell-Lama Affordability Crisis
2026-07-16.facts: Speaker Julie Menin and more than a dozen City Council members grilled the Housing Preservation and Development Department Thursday over rising costs at Mitchell-Lama properties—subsidized housing that's supposed to stay affordable. The hearing drew pointed questions about why costs are climbing across an aging portfolio of buildings that were designed to remain within reach for middle-class New Yorkers.

2026-07-16.title: Mamdani Administration Proposes Tougher Penalties for Landlords
2026-07-16.facts: Mayor Zohran Mamdani's administration released a 67-page plan Thursday to crack down on landlords who violate housing codes—and it's not subtle. The proposal comes out of "Rental Ripoff" hearings held earlier this year, where the city heard from 2,400 renters about their experiences with landlords.

2026-07-16.title: Argentina Stuns England With Last-Gasp Goals to Reach World Cup Final
2026-07-16.facts: Argentina pulled off one of the most dramatic comebacks in World Cup history Tuesday night, scoring twice in the final five minutes to beat England 2-1 and advance to Sunday's final against Spain. The reigning champions were staring at elimination until the 85th minute, when they flipped the match on its head at Mercedes-Benz Stadium.

2026-07-16.title: Timberwolves Acquire LaMelo Ball in Blockbuster Trade
2026-07-16.facts: Minnesota just landed one of the league's most electrifying guards. The Timberwolves have acquired LaMelo Ball in what team president Tim Connelly is calling a franchise-altering move, telling reporters the point guard could "elevate" the team's ceiling.

2026-07-16.title: NBA Commissioner Sets Deadline for Kawhi Leonard Investigation
2026-07-16.facts: Adam Silver just put a timeline on one of the league's most high-profile investigations. The NBA commissioner said the probe into Kawhi Leonard for alleged salary cap circumvention must wrap up before next season tips off.

2026-07-16.title: Anthropic Launches Free Claude for Teachers—and Stride's Stock Takes a Hit
2026-07-16.facts: Anthropic just opened up Claude to every K-12 teacher in the United States, completely free. The program's called Claude for Teachers, and it gives educators full access to the AI assistant for lesson planning, grading, personalized learning materials—pretty much anything classroom-related.

2026-07-17.title: Britain's Next Prime Minister Emerges From the North
2026-07-17.facts: Andy Burnham is about to become the most powerful politician in Britain—and he's done it from outside London. The plain-spoken Labour politician from northwest England is set to be named party leader on Friday, which effectively makes him prime minister apparent given Labour's position.

2026-07-17.title: U.S. Launches Fresh Strikes on Iranian Infrastructure
2026-07-17.facts: American warplanes hit bridges and a port in southern Iran overnight, according to Iranian media—though the Pentagon's telling a different story about what was targeted. U.S.

2026-07-17.title: Trump's Election Evidence Release Falls Flat
2026-07-17.facts: President Trump promised bombshell proof of election tampering and Chinese interference in the 2020 election—but the documents his administration released this week didn't deliver. The materials, which Trump claimed would validate his longstanding allegations about the integrity of that race, appear to have disappointed even those hoping for major revelations.

2026-07-17.title: Trump Administration Targets Brazil With New Tariffs
2026-07-17.facts: The White House is slapping fresh tariffs on Brazil, accusing the country of unfair trade practices in a move that replaces earlier duties struck down by the Supreme Court. The administration hasn't specified which Brazilian goods will face higher import taxes or what the rates will be, but the timing suggests this is part of a broader push to rewrite America's trade relationships.

2026-07-17.title: The Hidden Cost of a More Dangerous World
2026-07-17.facts: Global instability is about to hit your wallet in ways most people haven't thought about yet. Businesses operating in what they're calling a "more dangerous and unpredictable world" are facing sharply higher costs for everything from insurance to security to rerouting supply chains away from conflict zones.

2026-07-17.title: Britain Nationalizes Its Last Virgin Steel Plant
2026-07-17.facts: The UK government has taken British Steel into public ownership, nationalizing the country's only remaining plant that produces virgin steel from raw materials. The move comes after years of uncertainty over the facility's future and marks a dramatic intervention in a sector London says is a "vital national capability." Virgin steel production—making steel from iron ore rather than recycling scrap—is seen as strategically essential, especially for defense and infrastructure projects that require high-grade material.

2026-07-17.title: Semiconductor Slump Opens Door for AI Infrastructure Winners
2026-07-17.facts: Chip stocks are taking a beating, but there's another corner of the artificial intelligence boom that's quietly thriving—and investors are starting to pay attention. While semiconductor giants have slumped in recent weeks, companies that build the infrastructure supporting AI are posting strong gains.

2026-07-17.title: NoMad Parking Garage Sells for $34 Million in Likely Development Play
2026-07-17.facts: A parking garage in NoMad just changed hands for $33.5 million, and it's a safe bet those eight stories won't be storing cars much longer. The property at 10 East 30th Street sold from Aero Park Corporation to Royal Yacht Tower Management in what appears to be part of a larger development play.

2026-07-17.title: City Hall Rebrands Landlords as "High Road" and "Low Road" Operators
2026-07-17.facts: The Mamdani administration has a new vocabulary for talking about landlords, and it's tucked into page 43 of the city's latest "Rental Ripoff" report. Instead of calling out "bad" or "negligent" landlords, City Hall now divides the industry into two camps: "high road" and "low road." High-road landlords, according to the administration, meet regularly with tenants, work with officials and lenders to maintain their buildings, and keep "modest returns stable." Low-road landlords, by contrast, are defined as speculators on distressed properties—though the report's description cuts off there.

2026-07-17.title: Chase Burns Signs $105 Million Extension With Reds
2026-07-17.facts: Chase Burns isn't going anywhere. The Reds' All-Star right-hander just locked in a seven-year, $105 million contract extension, keeping him in Cincinnati through 2032.

2026-07-17.title: NBA Investigating Bucks' $64 Million Deal With Gary Trent Jr.
2026-07-17.facts: The league's opened an investigation into a four-year, $64 million contract between Gary Trent Jr. and the Milwaukee Bucks, looking into whether the team bent salary cap rules to get it done.

2026-07-17.title: Jonas Valanciunas Leaves NBA to Return Home to Lithuania
2026-07-17.facts: Jonas Valanciunas is heading home. The big man signed a two-year contract with Zalgiris Kaunas, the Lithuanian club announced Wednesday, walking away from the NBA after more than a decade in the league.

2026-07-17.title: Claude Can Now Log You In Anywhere—Without Ever Seeing Your Password
2026-07-17.facts: Claude just got a real-world superpower: it can now authenticate on your behalf using 1Password, logging into websites and services without your credentials ever passing through the model itself. The integration—announced today—means Claude can handle tasks that used to require you to manually copy-paste passwords or interrupt an autonomous workflow to type in a login.

2026-07-18.title: Indian Police Forcibly End Activist's Three-Week Hunger Strike
2026-07-18.facts: Indian police have forcibly removed and hospitalized Sonam Wangchuk after a hunger strike that stretched into its twentieth day. The activist began his strike last month to protest what he calls educational mismanagement that's affected millions of students across the country.

2026-07-18.title: India Launches First Commercial Rocket Built for Orbit
2026-07-18.facts: India's space industry just hit a milestone that signals its arrival as a commercial player. Skyroot Aerospace, the country's first space-tech unicorn, successfully launched its Vikram-1 rocket on Saturday—India's first commercial rocket designed to reach orbit.

2026-07-18.title: Trump Administration Loses Hundreds of Criminal Cases Against ICE Protesters
2026-07-18.facts: The Trump administration has lost or abandoned hundreds of criminal cases it brought against protesters and immigrants who allegedly interfered with ICE operations, according to a New York Times investigation. The cases are crumbling across the country, with prosecutors dropping charges or judges dismissing them for lack of evidence.

2026-07-18.title: New Russia Sanctions Bill Could Undermine Dollar's Global Role
2026-07-18.facts: Congress just passed a bill broadening sanctions on Russia, but there's a catch that's making economists and Treasury officials nervous—it might accelerate efforts by adversaries to move away from the U.S. dollar.

2026-07-18.title: America's Startup Boom Ends Decades-Long Drought
2026-07-18.facts: After a decades-long slump, hundreds of thousands of Americans are launching new companies each year in what's shaping up to be a genuine entrepreneurial resurgence. The surge in new business formation marks a sharp reversal from the sluggish trends that dominated much of the 2000s and early 2010s.

2026-07-18.title: War Risk Pushes Up Prices Across the Global Economy
2026-07-18.facts: Operating in a more dangerous and unpredictable world is about to cost businesses—and ultimately consumers—a lot more money. Companies are now pricing in geopolitical risk in ways they haven't had to for decades, and that's going to push up the cost of everything from food to electronics.

2026-07-18.title: China's Moonshot AI Closes Gap With Silicon Valley Giants
2026-07-18.facts: A Chinese artificial intelligence startup called Moonshot AI just unveiled a massive new model—Kimi K3—that it claims can go toe-to-toe with top American firms like OpenAI and Anthropic. The release is raising uncomfortable questions in Silicon Valley about whether the U.S.

2026-07-18.title: SpaceX Shares Tumble After Launch Scrubbed
2026-07-18.facts: SpaceX shares closed below their IPO price for the second straight day Friday, capping a rough week that saw the stock lose about four percent in premarket trading alone. The slide came after the company aborted a crucial launch, though details on which mission was scrubbed weren't disclosed.

2026-07-18.title: Greenpoint Megaproject Gets Final Approval After Affordable Housing Deal
2026-07-18.facts: The Gotham Organization just cleared its final hurdle to build two residential towers in Brooklyn's Greenpoint—and it took a major concession to get there. The City Council gave the green light Thursday for 1,324 apartments at 40-56 Quay Street, right on the waterfront, after the developer agreed to make half the units affordable housing.

2026-07-18.title: Structural Failure at Pfizer Tower Traced to Missing Steel Reinforcement
2026-07-18.facts: Engineers now know what went wrong at the Pfizer Building conversion that forced a blocks-wide evacuation in Midtown last week. Construction workers apparently failed to install critical steel reinforcement plates that were supposed to prevent two columns from buckling—and those columns collapsed on July 7 at the 37-story office-to-residential project at 235 East 42nd Street.

2026-07-18.title: Aston Villa Signs World Cup Star Manzambi in Record Swiss Deal
2026-07-18.facts: Aston Villa just pulled off a major coup, signing Johan Manzambi fresh off his breakout performance at the World Cup. The Swiss midfielder's move to the Champions League-bound club set a new record for a transfer involving a Swiss player, though the exact fee hasn't been disclosed.

2026-07-18.title: NBA Investigates Bucks Deal With Gary Trent Jr.
2026-07-18.facts: The league is looking into whether the Milwaukee Bucks and Gary Trent Jr. broke salary cap rules with their four-year, $64 million contract.

2026-07-18.title: Meta in Talks to Lease Computing Power to Anthropic in $10 Billion Deal
2026-07-18.facts: Meta and Anthropic are negotiating what could become one of the largest infrastructure deals in AI history—a potential $10 billion agreement for Meta to lease massive amounts of its computing power to the company behind Claude. The deal would give Anthropic access to Meta's enormous GPU clusters, solving one of the biggest bottlenecks facing AI companies right now: securing enough chips to train and run cutting-edge models.

2026-07-19.title: Ferry Disaster Off Guyana Coast
2026-07-19.facts: A passenger ferry carrying 116 people has capsized off the coast of Guyana, sparking a frantic rescue operation in the waters between Georgetown and Port Kaituma. Authorities confirmed that 53 people have been pulled from the water so far, but that leaves more than 60 passengers still unaccounted for.

2026-07-19.title: Tokyo's Shorts Debate Goes Viral
2026-07-19.facts: Tokyo's government just gave office workers permission to wear shorts to work for the first time this summer, and the announcement has sparked an unexpectedly heated national conversation about workplace etiquette and, yes, hairy legs. The policy change is aimed at helping employees cope with Japan's brutal summer heat and humidity, which regularly pushes temperatures into the mid-90s and has led to health warnings in recent years.

2026-07-19.title: Tate Brothers Arrested in Miami on U.K. Sex Crime Charges
2026-07-19.facts: Federal marshals arrested Andrew and Tristan Tate in Miami following charges filed by British authorities for sex crimes dating back to 2010. The influencer brothers, who've built massive online followings promoting misogynistic views, now face extradition to the United Kingdom after years of criminal investigations across Europe.

2026-07-19.title: Chicago Federal Prosecutors Flee Office Over Immigrant Cases
2026-07-19.facts: The U.S. attorney's office in Chicago is hemorrhaging prosecutors who say they're being forced to bring cases against protesters and immigrants that they believe are unjust.

2026-07-19.title: Trump Administration Shuttered Decades-Old Workplace Discrimination Office
2026-07-19.facts: The Trump administration quietly dismantled a little-known federal office that spent decades investigating potential discrimination by government contractors, effectively ending federal scrutiny of bias in a massive sector of the U.S. economy.

2026-07-19.title: Netflix Doubles Down on Sports as Subscriber Growth Stalls
2026-07-19.facts: Netflix is pouring serious money into live sports programming, betting it can reverse slowing subscriber growth—but Wall Street's not buying in just yet. The streaming giant says live events help attract new sign-ups, especially as competition from Disney Plus, Max, and others continues to intensify.

2026-07-19.title: British Steel Nationalized as Chinese Owner Threatens Legal Action
2026-07-19.facts: The UK government has nationalized British Steel, and the company's Chinese owner is threatening to fight back in court. Jingye Group, which acquired the steelmaker in 2020, says it'll pursue compensation "through legal means to the very end" after being forced out.

2026-07-19.title: New EU Border System Causing Massive Delays at European Airports
2026-07-19.facts: European airports are warning travelers to brace for much longer waits this summer, thanks to a new EU border control system that's tripling processing times at passport control. Airport executives say the enhanced checks are creating bottlenecks that weren't anticipated when the system was designed.

2026-07-19.title: Mamdani's New Landlord Labels Divide NYC Property Owners
2026-07-19.facts: Mayor Zohran Mamdani just rolled out his "Rental Ripoff Report" this week, and it's doing something clever: instead of vaguely attacking "negligent landlords," it's sorting property owners into two camps. You're either a "high-road landlord" who responds to tenant complaints and works with the city, or you're a "low-road landlord" who speculates on rent-stabilized buildings using high-risk financial strategies.

2026-07-19.title: Spain and Argentina Meet in World Cup Final
2026-07-19.facts: The World Cup final is set, and it's a clash that's been building for weeks: Spain against Argentina, with all eyes on the generational duel between Lamine Yamal and Lionel Messi. The match caps off a tournament that's seen both teams navigate grueling knockout rounds to reach Sunday's showdown.

2026-07-19.title: Orioles Lock Up Kyle Bradish with Ninety Million Dollar Extension
2026-07-19.facts: The Baltimore Orioles have signed starting pitcher Kyle Bradish to a five-year extension worth $90 million, locking down one of their rotation anchors through 2029. It's a significant investment in a pitcher who's become central to Baltimore's resurgence as a contender in the American League East.

2026-07-19.title: Mavericks Match Knicks' Offer to Keep Cisse in Dallas
2026-07-19.facts: The Dallas Mavericks are keeping Moussa Cisse after matching a two-year, $4.7 million offer sheet the New York Knicks extended to the restricted free agent. Cisse's agents confirmed the move to ESPN's Shams Charania, ending what could have been an unusual front-office chess match between the two franchises.

2026-07-19.title: Fable 5 Gets Permanent Home in Claude Max, Loses Casual Access in Pro
2026-07-19.facts: Anthropic just ended the limbo around Fable 5—its most powerful model—and the new structure is all about pushing users toward the tier that matches their usage. If you're on Claude Max or Team Premium, Fable 5 is now a permanent fixture in your subscription, though it'll run at 50% of your normal usage limits.

2026-07-20.title: India's Medical Exam Scandal Sparks Mass Protest
2026-07-20.facts: Millions of Indian students have been forced to retake the country's biggest medical school entrance exam after the questions leaked ahead of test day. The scandal triggered mass demonstrations led by the CJP Party, which organized a protest march to Parliament to demand accountability.

2026-07-20.title: Norway Unveils Memorial for 2011 Massacre Victims
2026-07-20.facts: Norway opened a new memorial in Oslo on Sunday, just ahead of the 15th anniversary of one of the country's darkest days. The artwork honors victims of the 2011 massacre, an attack that shook the normally peaceful Scandinavian nation to its core.

2026-07-20.title: China's Blue-Collar Writer Wins Top Literary Prize
2026-07-20.facts: A food delivery rider just won China's most prestigious literary prize, and it's shining a spotlight on a corner of Chinese society that usually stays invisible. Wang Jibing, who works delivering meals on a motorbike, is part of a growing movement of blue-collar Chinese workers who are gaining wider attention for their writing.

2026-07-20.title: Turkey Expands Military Ties with Eastern Libya
2026-07-20.facts: Turkey is deepening military cooperation with Khalifa Haftar, the warlord who controls eastern Libya, and the move is raising serious questions about regional security and Egypt's tolerance. Haftar has long been backed by Egypt, which views eastern Libya as a buffer zone and has drawn red lines against foreign military expansion there.

2026-07-20.title: Pentagon Concealed Dozens of U.S. Military Injuries in Iran Conflict
2026-07-20.facts: The Pentagon has been withholding information about dozens of U.S. military personnel injured in operations related to Iran, a military official acknowledged Monday.

2026-07-20.title: Massachusetts Mother Stands Trial for Deaths of Three Children
2026-07-20.facts: Lindsay Clancy goes on trial today in a case that's forced the small coastal town of Duxbury, Massachusetts, to confront an unthinkable tragedy. The mother is charged with murdering her three children, and the trial is expected to center heavily on questions of postpartum mental health.

2026-07-20.title: Utah Flash Flood Kills Fire Captain and His Family of Five
2026-07-20.facts: A Provo fire captain, his wife, and their three sons were found dead over the weekend in southern Utah after flash floods swept through a canyon near their campground. Authorities say the family of five was likely caught in floodwaters triggered by severe storms that hammered the region Saturday.

2026-07-20.title: Inflation Spreads Beyond Isolated Shocks
2026-07-20.facts: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh told Congress last week that one of his main jobs is to prevent individual price spikes from spreading across the economy. Unfortunately, that's already happening.

2026-07-20.title: Ryanair Profits Tumble as Iran War Drives Up Fuel Costs
2026-07-20.facts: Ryanair's profits just took a hit, and the airline is pointing to two culprits: war jitters and soaring fuel costs. The Irish carrier reported a drop in earnings as the conflict in Iran has kept passengers at home and pushed oil prices higher.

2026-07-20.title: Thames Water Lenders Ready Legal Fight Over Nationalisation Threat
2026-07-20.facts: Creditors holding billions in Thames Water debt are preparing to sue if Britain's new government tries to take the troubled utility into public hands. The BBC reports lenders are gearing up for a legal challenge that would demand full repayment of outstanding loans—a figure running into the multi-billions of pounds.

2026-07-20.title: Chip Stocks Enter Bear Territory as Investors Hunt for New Plays
2026-07-20.facts: Semiconductors just fell into a bear market, and Wall Street's scrambling to figure out what comes next. The sector that powered much of the AI rally has now dropped enough to meet the official definition of a bear market, leaving investors who rode the chip boom looking for safer ground.

2026-07-20.title: New York Mayor Rolls Out Tenant Reform Plan After Rental Complaint Hearings
2026-07-20.facts: Mayor Zohran Mamdani released his administration's first major housing enforcement plan Thursday, responding to what he's calling widespread complaints from New York City renters. The initiative follows the mayor's Rental Ripoff Hearings, where tenants testified about conditions and landlord issues across the city.

2026-07-20.title: Spain Claims Second World Cup Title in Extra-Time Thriller
2026-07-20.facts: Spain are world champions again. Ferran Torres scored the winner in extra time to give Spain their second men's World Cup title, defeating reigning champions Argentina in a final that turned tense and fractious at MetLife Stadium on Sunday.

2026-07-20.title: Thunder Trade All-Defensive Wing Dort to Hawks in Three-Team Deal
2026-07-20.facts: The Thunder just shook up their roster, trading All-Defensive wing Luguentz Dort to the Hawks in a three-team deal that's sending ripples across the league. Oklahoma City is getting three second-round picks in return, while the Mavericks land former number one overall pick Zaccharie Risacher and Atlanta also receives Ryan Nembhard.

2026-07-20.title: Claude Fable 5 Moves Behind Paywall for Pro Users
2026-07-20.facts: Anthropic just pulled the plug on free access to Claude Fable 5 for Pro subscribers, and starting July 20th, you'll need to upgrade to a higher-tier plan if you want to keep using their most powerful model. The change marks a significant shift in how Anthropic is monetizing its flagship AI, moving what had been a Pro subscription perk into a more expensive tier.

2026-07-21.title: Ferry Disaster Off Guyana Leaves Dozens Dead or Missing
2026-07-21.facts: At least 27 people are dead and 83 are still missing after a passenger ferry capsized off the coast of Guyana. The island nation's prime minister has launched an investigation into the tragedy and says he's holding out hope that more survivors will be found alive.

2026-07-21.title: Manila Reports Sailor Injured in South China Sea Clash
2026-07-21.facts: The Philippines says one of its sailors was injured during a confrontation with the Chinese Coast Guard in disputed waters of the South China Sea. China's pushing back, insisting it wasn't the aggressor in the encounter.

2026-07-21.title: Five Bodies Pulled from Ohio River After Child's Desperate Alert
2026-07-21.facts: A child flagged down a driver with a horrifying message: "My family was in the river." That led authorities to pull five bodies from the Scioto River outside Columbus on Tuesday. Two women and three men were recovered from the water, though officials haven't yet released their identities or explained what led to their deaths.

2026-07-21.title: Trump Imposes 50% Tariff on Canadian Goods
2026-07-21.facts: The Trump administration is slapping a 50 percent tariff on many Canadian exports, reigniting one of the sharpest trade confrontations between the two countries in decades. The duties will use an untested legal provision, marking new territory in how the U.S.

2026-07-21.title: Fed Chair Warsh Still Silent on Rate Strategy
2026-07-21.facts: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh appeared before Congress again this week and once more declined to offer any concrete plan for how he'll tackle inflation. Warsh, who took over the Fed earlier this year, has now made multiple public appearances where he's pledged to bring down price pressures without explaining whether that means raising interest rates, holding them steady, or trying something altogether different.

2026-07-21.title: UK Cuts Electricity Tax to Lower Household Bills
2026-07-21.facts: Britain is slashing the VAT on household electricity starting in October, a move that'll save homes around 45 pounds a year. The tax cut was announced on Prime Minister Andy Burnham's first full day in office, signaling an early focus on cost-of-living relief.

2026-07-21.title: Morgan Stanley Slashes Adobe Stock Rating on AI Competition Fears
2026-07-21.facts: Morgan Stanley just delivered a brutal downgrade to Adobe, slashing its price target by more than a third and cutting the stock to underweight. The investment bank's citing multiple transitions at the software giant and growing threats from artificial intelligence competitors that could eat into Adobe's dominance.

2026-07-21.title: City Clarifies Controversial Pied-à-Terre Tax After Flood of Questions
2026-07-21.facts: The Mamdani administration's pied-à-terre tax just got a dozen pages of updates and clarifications after the city was swamped with questions from property owners and their lawyers. The Department of Finance released new text amendments Monday aimed at clearing up confusion about who's actually subject to the surcharge and how the appeals process works.

2026-07-21.title: MetroLoft's Conversion Method Under Scrutiny After Midtown Evacuation
2026-07-21.facts: A dramatic structural failure at a Midtown conversion project has put MetroLoft's signature redevelopment technique under a microscope. Columns buckled and floors sagged earlier this month at 235 East 42nd Street—the former Pfizer headquarters that MetroLoft is converting—forcing authorities to temporarily evacuate the area.

2026-07-21.title: Packers Lock Down Defensive Star Devonte Wyatt with $57 Million Extension
2026-07-21.facts: The Green Bay Packers just made sure one of their defensive anchors isn't going anywhere. Defensive lineman Devonte Wyatt has signed a three-year, $57 million contract extension that includes a $20 million signing bonus, his agency confirmed Monday.

2026-07-21.title: Thunder Trade All-Defensive Wing Dort in Three-Team Deal
2026-07-21.facts: The Thunder just dealt Luguentz Dort to the Atlanta Hawks in a three-team blockbuster that's reshaping rosters across the league. Oklahoma City's sending its All-Defensive wing out after years anchoring their perimeter defense, and in return they're collecting three second-round picks to add to what's already one of the deepest draft capital stashes in the NBA.

2026-07-21.title: Anthropic Settles AI Copyright Lawsuit for $1.5 Billion
2026-07-21.facts: A federal judge has approved a massive $1.5 billion settlement between Anthropic and a coalition of publishers and authors who accused the company of training Claude on copyrighted material without permission. It's the largest copyright settlement in AI history, dwarfing previous deals in the industry by a significant margin.

2026-07-22.title: Mexico's Lettuce Growers Caught in Food Safety Crossfire
2026-07-22.facts: A lettuce-growing region in central Mexico is now at the center of a food safety crisis that's sickened thousands of Americans. U.S.

2026-07-22.title: Japan Debates Work Culture After PM's Extreme Sleep Schedule
2026-07-22.facts: Japan's prime minister has sparked a national conversation about work-life balance, and it's not in the way you'd expect. Sanae Takaichi revealed she sleeps just "zero to three hours" a night, and the admission has startled the country.

2026-07-22.title: Saudi Arabia Gains U.S. Approval for Nuclear Fuel Enrichment
2026-07-22.facts: The United States has struck a deal with Saudi Arabia that would allow the kingdom to enrich its own nuclear fuel, a threshold that's sparked sharp opposition in Washington and Tel Aviv. The agreement, set to be announced Wednesday, centers on what Riyadh calls a civilian nuclear energy program.

2026-07-22.title: Trump Ally Wins GOP Nomination for Arizona Governor
2026-07-22.facts: Representative Andy Biggs has won the Republican nomination for governor of Arizona, setting up a high-stakes general election battle in one of the country's most closely watched swing states. Biggs, a conservative congressman and close ally of President Trump, emerged from a contentious primary to take on Democratic incumbent Katie Hobbs.

2026-07-22.title: Trump to Hit Canada with 50% Tariffs, Reigniting Trade War
2026-07-22.facts: President Trump's about to pull the trigger on a massive new round of tariffs against Canada—this time hitting many Canadian goods with a punishing 50 percent duty. The administration plans to use an untested legal provision to justify the move, which would reignite a major clash with one of America's largest trading partners.

2026-07-22.title: Oil Surges to Six-Week High as Iran Hopes Fade
2026-07-22.facts: Crude oil prices just hit their highest level in six weeks, climbing nearly 4 percent on Wednesday as hopes for any de-escalation in Iran evaporated. Both West Texas Intermediate and Brent crude saw sharp gains after an eleventh consecutive night of attacks in the region, signaling that the conflict isn't winding down anytime soon.

2026-07-22.title: Tokyo Tells Workers to Ditch Suits for Shorts—But Some Women Call It Harassment
2026-07-22.facts: Tokyo's government has launched a campaign urging office workers to swap their suits and ties for t-shirts, trainers, and shorts—but the push is sparking an unexpected backlash. The Tokyo Metropolitan Government says the dress-down policy is meant to help workers cope with the city's brutal summer heat and reduce energy use by cutting air conditioning.

2026-07-22.title: AT&T Shares Climb on Strong Subscriber Growth
2026-07-22.facts: AT&T beat Wall Street's expectations across the board, sending its stock higher as investors cheered stronger-than-expected subscriber additions and cash flow. The telecommunications company reported better-than-forecast numbers on new customers signing up for its wireless and fiber services, a sign that its network investments are paying off.

2026-07-22.title: City Hall Wants Voters to Decide on Permit Overhaul This Fall
2026-07-22.facts: The Mamdani administration's charter commission is putting together a ballot measure that could reshape how New York City handles construction permits and government contracts. The proposal would create a one-stop shop for builders to get permits, instead of forcing them to navigate multiple agencies.

2026-07-22.title: Dolphins Lock Up Brooks with $51 Million Extension
2026-07-22.facts: The Miami Dolphins just handed linebacker Jordyn Brooks a major payday, locking him in with a three-year extension worth $51.3 million. The deal includes $35 million guaranteed, agent Erik Burkhardt confirmed to ESPN.

2026-07-22.title: Hawks Land Dort in Blockbuster Three-Team Trade
2026-07-22.facts: The Hawks just pulled off one of the summer's most surprising moves, acquiring All-Defensive wing Luguentz Dort from Oklahoma City in a three-team deal that reshapes rosters across the league. Atlanta sent former number one overall pick Zaccharie Risacher to Dallas and Ryan Nembhard to Oklahoma City as part of the swap.

2026-07-22.title: Anthropic Settles $1.5 Billion Copyright Lawsuit Over Training Data
2026-07-22.facts: A federal judge has approved Anthropic's $1.5 billion settlement with authors and publishers who accused the company of using pirated books to train Claude without permission. The deal closes one of the largest copyright cases yet filed against an AI company, ending a legal battle that had been building since the suit was filed.

2026-07-23.title: Ebola Outbreak Spreads Through Congolese Displacement Camps
2026-07-23.facts: A million people displaced by violence in Congo are now facing an Ebola outbreak with almost no resources to fight it. The virus is spreading through overcrowded camps in the epicenter of the outbreak, where clean water is scarce and conditions are dangerous.

2026-07-23.title: Wildfires Claim Third Firefighter as France Orders Mass Evacuations
2026-07-23.facts: France has evacuated thousands of residents as wildfires continue burning across Europe, and the human cost just got higher—a third firefighter has now died battling the blazes. The fires are part of a broader pattern hitting multiple countries on the continent, straining firefighting resources across national borders.

2026-07-23.title: Russian Spy Flees Japan After Exposure in Aeroflot Office
2026-07-23.facts: A Russian military intelligence officer has fled Japan after being publicly identified as using the country's Aeroflot airline office in Tokyo as cover for espionage operations. The Times reported the officer's identity and activities, essentially blowing his cover and forcing him to leave the country.

2026-07-23.title: Over 3,200 Israelis Storm Al-Aqsa Mosque Compound
2026-07-23.facts: More than 3,200 Israelis entered the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound in Jerusalem's Old City, accompanied by National Security Minister Ben-Gvir and far-right groups under heavy police protection. The incursion into one of Islam's holiest sites is the kind of event that can spark wider unrest across the region.

2026-07-23.title: Minnesota Gunman Faces Life Sentence for Attacks on Lawmakers
2026-07-23.facts: The Minnesota man who shot state lawmakers in their homes last year is expected to be sentenced to life in prison. The attacks, which targeted legislators at their residences, shocked the state and raised urgent questions about political violence and security for elected officials.

2026-07-23.title: Trump Unveils 50 Percent Tariffs on Canada as Trade War Escalates
2026-07-23.facts: The White House just announced that 50 percent tariffs on Canadian goods will take effect by August 19th, marking a dramatic escalation in a trade dispute that's now stretched well over a year. It's the most aggressive move yet in what's become an increasingly bitter economic standoff between Washington and Ottawa.

2026-07-23.title: Oil Climbs Toward $100 as Houthis Strike Saudi Tankers in Red Sea
2026-07-23.facts: Oil prices surged past $98 a barrel Thursday after Houthi rebels claimed responsibility for attacks on Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea. Brent crude jumped on fears that escalating attacks on shipping could tighten global supply and drive crude toward the $100 mark for the first time in months.

2026-07-23.title: Google's AI Spending Spree Drains Cash as Costs Spiral
2026-07-23.facts: Google's burning through cash at a staggering rate, and artificial intelligence is the reason why. The tech giant said earlier this year it expects to spend as much as $190 billion on AI investments, a figure that's raising eyebrows even in an industry known for massive capital outlays.

2026-07-23.title: Landlords Challenge Rent Freeze in Court
2026-07-23.facts: Six New York landlords have filed suit in state Supreme Court asking a judge to throw out last month's rent freeze for stabilized apartments and force the Rent Guidelines Board to authorize an increase instead. The petition argues the board's consideration of a freeze was a predetermined outcome and what they're calling a "sham" process.

2026-07-23.title: JPMorgan Exits Third Avenue Tower in $425 Million Deal
2026-07-23.facts: Fisher Brothers is about to recapitalize one of its major Midtown office towers, and it comes with a hefty price tag. JPMorgan Asset Management is selling its 49 percent stake in 605 Third Avenue, the 43-story tower near Grand Central, at a valuation of $425 million.

2026-07-23.title: Dolphins Lock Up Brooks With $51 Million Extension
2026-07-23.facts: The Dolphins just handed linebacker Jordyn Brooks one of the richest deals at his position. Miami and Brooks have agreed to a three-year, $51.3 million extension with $35 million guaranteed, his agent confirmed to ESPN.

2026-07-23.title: Curry Gets His Own Hall of Fame Exhibit While Still Playing
2026-07-23.facts: Stephen Curry's making history before he's even retired. The Warriors star becomes the first active player to get his own exhibit at the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame, opening to the public Friday.

2026-07-23.title: Claude Can Now Screen-Record Its Way to Expertise
2026-07-23.facts: Anthropic just shipped something that changes how you train Claude on complex workflows: the AI can now watch a screen recording of you doing a task—navigating software, processing documents, whatever—and learn the entire process from the video. This isn't about transcribing what you say or reading static screenshots; Claude's analyzing the full motion, clicks, navigation patterns, and context switches in real time.
